arcBallGeometry.summary: Similar to the Palette:arcBallCamera component, the arcBallGeometry can be used to interactively control a geometry's position in a scene with a fixed camera. The actual functional parts are provided packaged in a complete render setup to better illustrate its usage. You can copy the Geometry COMP arcGeo out of this component and use it in your own render scene or use this as a default interactive render setup. All parameters described here are located on the actual Geometry COMP . The geometry can be rotated ( lclick ), dollied ( mclick ) and translated ( rclick ).

audioAnalysis.summary: A component to analyze an input audio waveform for its properties such as Low-, Mid-, High-Level, Kick and Snare detection, Rythm, Spectral Centroid as well as Slow- and Fast- Spectral Density.

audioSet.summary: The audioSet COMP is a visualization and modifier of gain in an Audio File In CHOP .

autoMediaPlayer.summary: The autoMediaPlayer allows for looping over movies and still images in a specified folder on disc in linear or randomized order. There are options to adjust the output resolution, change the length of still images, as well as set the transition length.

battery.summary: The Battery COMP monitors the battery or power status of your machine. A Perform CHOP is used to get the power status of your machine.

bitwigClipSlot.summary: The bitwigClipSlot COMP serves as an interface for controlling clip slot playback and receiving playback information of all clip slots for a given track. Users begin by choosing which track they are interested in; from there they can access the clip slots associated with that track, and subsequently decide to perform actions on a specific clip slot.

bitwigClip.summary: The bitwigClip COMP serves as an interface for controlling a specific launcher clip in Bitwig's clip launcher context. Users begin by choosing which track they are interested in; then the launcher clip, and subsequently decide to perform actions or read/modify the properties of a given launcher clip.

bitwigMain.summary: The BitwigMain COMP acts as the central communication and information hub for the TDBitwig System. It is responsible for relaying information between the Bitwig controller extension and the TDBitwig system. There should only be one of these Components present for every Bitwig session the user is communicating to; in most cases this will be one.

bitwigNote.summary: The bitwigNote COMP allows users to read a live stream of note events on a given Bitwig Track. The incoming notes will correspond to the track's note input source, whether it be output from a midi device or side-chained from another track or device within Bitwig. Users will use the navigation controls to specify which track's input source they want to monitor.

bitwigRemotesDevice.summary: The bitwigRemotesDevice COMP serves as an interface for accessing and controlling Device Remote Controls in Bitwig. Navigation to controls begins by selecting a track, followed by the device within that track, and ending with the desired page. The 8 Remote Control Parameters on this Component will correspond to the 8 Remote Controls on a single page.

bitwigRemotesProject.summary: The bitwigRemotesProject COMP serves as an interface for accessing and controlling global Project Remote Controls in Bitwig. Navigation to controls begins by selecting the desired page. The 8 Remote Control Parameters on this Component will correspond to the 8 Remote Controls on a single page.

bitwigRemotesTrack.summary: The bitwigRemotesTrack COMP serves as an interface for accessing and controlling Track Remote Controls in Bitwig. Navigation to controls begins by selecting a track, followed by the desired page. The 8 Remote Control Parameters on this Component will correspond to the 8 Remote Controls on a single page.

bitwigSelect.summary: The bitwigSelect COMP enables users to retrieve channel information from the output CHOP of any Bitwig Component in a project. Component search can be filtered by the Object type, and once the ouput CHOP is selected, users can filter specific channels they are interested in. The selection of Components uses python references based on relative paths.

bitwigSong.summary: The bitwigSong COMP acts as a bi-directional interface for Bitwig's global transport; including timing, playback, and recording functionality. It also stores information about scenes and cues, and supports control for their playback

bitwigTrack.summary: The BitwigTrack COMP acts as an interface for accessing and controlling the attributes of a given Bitwig Track object. It allows users to choose the specific track they are interested in and perform any actions accordingly.

blendModes.summary: blendModes is a 2-stream video mixer for exploring and using the 45+ blend modes of the Composite TOP . Connect 2 video inputs, select a Mix Mode (Crossfade by default) and use the X or XY crossfader at the top-middle. On the top right is the blended output of the component.

bloom.summary: The Bloom COMP is a combination of several image effects that selects and remap the brightest pixels of a TOP . The pixels in the second input TOP are warped around the selection defined by the blur parameters. The blur parameters modifies the selection of the brightest pixels of the input 1 TOP . For example increasing the “Contrast” parameter would reshape the selection to narrow the range of brightness, selecting the brightest pixels. The glow parameters overlays the pixels selected in the blur parameters with the RGB values selected by the “Glow Color” parameters. A Ramp TOP with a color spectrum is the default second input for this COMP . The ramp can be replaced by attaching a TOP to the second input of the component, or the built-one ramp can be edited with the Open Ramp Parameters pulse.

callbacksHelper.summary: The callbacksHelper Component provides an easy setup for Python callbacks on your custom components. It installs and sets up parameters on your component for the CallbacksExt Extension , and provides a few other usability features.

cameraViewport.summary: The cameraViewport component is a modified Camera COMP that can be used to interactively control a camera's position/rotation in a 3D scene. It can be used instead of the default Camera COMP . Its viewer is an interactive Panel containing a rendered image from any Render TOP .

camSchnappr.summary: camSchnappr is a interactive mapping application entirely inspired by MAPAMOK, created by Kyle McDonald at the YCAM Interlab. With Kyle publishing the source code for MAPAMOK, we had a chance to look at it and convert it into TouchDesigner. For the original tool and source have a look here :

changeColor.summary: The changeColor COMP selects a color using the RGB values in the “Color to change” parameter and modifies the selected color using the HSV parameter values. The changeColor COMP can output a color corrected TOP image or an alpha mask.

changeToColor.summary: The changeToColor COMP selects a color with the “From Color” parameter and transforms it to the color specified in the “To Color” parameter. The COMP changes the hue, saturation and value of “From Color” to the “To Color”, the tolerance of the transformation can be adjusted with the hue, saturation and value tolerance parameters. The changeToColor COMP outputs a color corrected TOP image or an alpha mask.

checker.summary: The Checker COMP uses two RGBA colors to create a checker pattern TOP image.

colorThreshold.summary: Modifies a chosen color channel from a TOP by multiplying the original input by an inverse of the selected color channels.

compareComp.summary: This compares two components. It first identifies nodes that are unique to either of the two components.

convolve.summary: The convolve COMP is a simple input interface for the Convolve TOP . The three parameters in "Line 1", "Line 2" and "Line 3" are a convolution matrix which is used to create image effects like sharpen, blur and edge detection. See Convolve TOP for more details.

cornerPinPOP.summary: The Corner Pin POP component allows for perspective or bilinear deformation of a POP . It can be best compared in functionality to the Corner Pin TOP . It uses OpenCV's . findHomography () function to calculate the perspective transform matrix.

cornerPinSOP.summary: The Corner Pin SOP component allows for perspective or bilinear deformation of a SOP . It can be best compared in functionality to the Corner Pin TOP .

cppParsTemplateGen.summary: The CppParsTemplateGen COMP can be used to quickly generate parameters.cpp and parameters.h files and kickstart your Custom Operator projects. The parameters.cpp and parameters.h files are used to define the Custom Parameters on Custom OPs.

customAttributes.summary: This component helps with adding custom attributes to a SOP via python scripting. It duplicates functionality found in the Point SOP , Vertex SOP and Primitive SOP but can still be helpful when trying to find out how to add attributes via python.

debugControl.summary: This component facilitates experimentation with and control of the builtin debug statement. It is an operator based interface for the debug module .

dilate.summary: The Dialate COMP is an example of morphological image processing created with a GLSL TOP . This COMP has two types of processesses dilate and erode. The dialate effect adds pixels to the edge of groups of similar pixels. The erode effect removes pixels on the edges of groups of similar pixels. More information on morphological operation can be found on this wikipedia article .

domeViewer.summary: A component designed to previsualize dome specific content and / or generate a dome shell (in POPs) to be used to design dome specific content. This is based out of Fred Trétout's (anodin) InsideDome. The attached file is a sample using the Dome Viewer to generate dome specific effects. File:DomeViewer LinesDemo.toe

feedbackEdge.summary: The Feedback Edge COMP is a feedback image effect created using a Feedback, Blur, Edge, Level and Hsvadj TOPs.

feedback.summary: The feedback COMP is an example of the feedback TOP . The feedback COMP is used to create a fake motion blur by not clearing the buffer. For more information see the Feedback TOP . See OP Snippets and the Examples section for some networks detailing to use the Feedback TOP .

firmata.summary: Firmata is an Arduino sketch allowing for configuration and control of the Arduino using messages formatted as MIDI.

gestureCapture.summary: The gestureCapture COMP records movement and then displays the curves as (1) rendered lines and/or (2) a dot following the curves and/or (3) free-hand drawn shapes. The outputs of gestureCapture are (a) the rendered image, (b) a SOP containing all paths as polygonal lines, (c) a CHOP containing the merged lines and (d) number of lines. Internally you can access a CHOP and a SOP for every gesture recorded.

graphPlot.summary: graphPlot creates 2D graphs with data points or curves plotted over XY grids with controls over units, ranges, divisions and labels.

hsvBlur.summary: The hsvBlur COMP converts a TOP to use the HSV color space then blurs the HSV channels individually.

imageSearch.summary: ImageSearch uses the Custom Search interface of Google to find images which match the given search term.

julia.summary: The Julia COMP is a mapping of the Julia fractal set, more information about the Julia set can be found here .

kantanMapper.summary: Kantan Mapper is an interface overlaid on-top of an actual projection to enable the drawing, creation and placement of various shapes onto real life objects and the possibility to assign TOP Image Nodes to these shapes and masks.

kantanUVHelper.summary: The kantanUVHelper is a utility tool to make use of knatanMapper's second UV Map output. The UV map has a texture id encoded in the blue channel and kantanUVHelper uses this to map the right input texture to the correct area. The benefit of this is that kantanMapper can be disabled or even deleted if the UV map from the second output is saved or locked.

kinectCalibration.summary: THe kinectCalibrate component allows for calculating the intrinsics and extrinsics of a projector in relation to a Kinect device making it possible to project onto the scene captured by the kinect. The theory is the same as camSchnappr. Where camSchnappr has a 3D Object that mirrors a physical object, this component uses the point cloud as a real-world reference. Projecting a checkboard, the component detects the corners in the color image of the kinect and retireves the 3D positions of these checkerboard corners from the poincloud data. Both sets of data (2D positions of corners in projector image and 3D pos of corners in pointcloud) are the variables needed by openCV's calibrateCamera function to solve for the position of the camera - in this case the projector. The Kinect's position in this setup is assumed to be at the root.

kinectPointcloud.summary: The kinectPointCloud COMP generates a point cloud using depth textures and color data. The point cloud texture is a 32-bit floating point texture where RGB pixel values are XYZ pixel values relative to the color camera, in meters. By default the COMP uses the Kinect device as an input, to use other depth cameras connect the color and depth textures to input 1 and input 2 of the component. Using the Kinect with this COMP is only supported on Windows OS with the Kinect for Windows hardware. To use a Kinect 2 device you need to install the SDK or runtime from here .

leapPaint.summary: The leapPaint COMP uses the Leap Motion device and user's hands as a controller to draw in a TOP . There are two API versions for the Leap Motion device, some features are not supported on “Version 4 Orion” API for example “swipe”. More information on using the Leap Motion in TouchDesigner can be found on this page .

lightTunnel.summary: The lightTunnel COMP uses a GLSL Multi TOP to remap and distort the pixels in an input TOP . The lightTunnel selects a circle in the input TOP and extends the color of the edges to stretch across the rest of the image.

lister.summary: The lister custom Component builds on List COMP to make a multi-featured tool for listing complex data. Formatting of the lister is set up in the config Component , which starts out docked to the lister. Data can be provided via Python, parameter or table. Complex data and effects can be achieved using callbacks . NOTE: Many of lister's features require a basic knowledge of Python.

logger.summary: Starting with the first build released publicly on the 2023.10k branch , TouchDesigner now comes with its own Logger COMP .

mandelbrot.summary: The Mandelbrot COMP is a mapping of the Mandlebrot fractal set, more information about the Mandelbrot set can be found here .

materialDesignIcons.summary: The materialDesignIcons component allows for browsing and searching of the MaterialDesignIcons font. The currently included version contains over 6000 icons.

mesh.summary: The Mesh COMP creates a SOP based on the selected parametric equation in the "shape" parameter.

monochrome.summary: The monochrome COMP converts a TOP to grayscale colors. The intensity of each color channel can be adjusted with the red, blue and green parameters.

motionSense.summary: The motionSense COMP detects motion in a TOP texture or a camera device. This COMP outputs a 0 or 1 in a CHOP if there is movement detected.

movieEngine.summary: The movieEngine Palette component is a UI-less moviePlayer , and is a multi-purpose tool for playing, controlling and examining movies. It can be used standalone or embedded in TouchDesigner applications. Unless otherwise noted, movieEngine features are also part of moviePlayer.

moviePlayer.summary: The moviePlayer Palette component (and the UI-less movieEngine ) is a multi-purpose tool for playing, controlling and examining movies. It can be used standalone or embedded in TouchDesigner applications.

moviePlaylist.summary: The moviePlaylist is able to loop over images or movies selected via a folder or specified via a Table DAT given as an input. It offers some control over playback, blend- and end- behaviour.

multiLevel.summary: By clicking and dragging around the UI, multiLevel outputs a single channel of multiple samples, or multiple channels one sample each.

noise.summary: The Noise COMP generates a variety of noise patterns based on the selected noise type. More information about how the noise patterns are calculated can be found on this blog by Brian Sharpe .

opBrowser.summary: The opBrowser Custom Component provides a range of network browsing, viewing, and parameter editing capabilities. It can be used as a standalone browser or embedded into other UI's. All configuration features are controlled by custom parameters and can be made accessible or not in the browser UI.

operatorPath.summary: The operatorPath Component is a full-featured UI element for navigating operator paths in TouchDesigner networks.

opticalFlow.summary: This finds apparent motion and puts the left-right motion in the red channel of the output TOP , and the bottom-top motion in the green channel.

particlesGpu.summary: particlesGpu is a compute shader based particle system.

pixelate.summary: The pixelate COMP turns a TOP to a color bitmap by adjust the resolution.

pixelRelocator.summary: The pixelRelocator COMP repositions the pixels from the first input TOP to a position determined by the second input TOP . The positions are determined by the color channels selected in the Horizontal source and vertical source parameter.

pointillize.summary: The pointillize COMP draws a point at each pixel and colors the point with the corresponding color, creating a pointillism effect.

pointRender.summary: Renders a point cloud from the data stored in the input textures. The first input should contain XYZ data stored in the RGB channels with the alpha channel set to one for pixels that contain valid points. The second input can contain optional color data that will be applied to each point. The Normalize Colors parameter can be used to rescale colors that are outside the expected zero to one range.

popDialog.summary: The popDialog custom Component builds on Container COMP to make an easy-to-use pop-up dialog for basic notifications and queries. The dialog can be set up using parameters or script.

popMenu.summary: The popMenu custom Component builds on Container COMP to make an easy-to-use pop-up menu that can be used as a context menu, in dropdown controls, or for other menu needs. The dialog can be set up using parameters or script. Multi-level menus can be created through scripting. Custom formatting of popMenu is set up in the internal config Component . A callback system is provided for more complex needs.

probe.summary: Probe is a visual performance monitor for TouchDesigner processes, measuring CPU times (circles), and GPU times (diamonds), plus, via the selector on the left, CPU memory, GPU memory and the number of children in a component.

pushPins.summary: The pushPins COMP creates a pushpin effect using a TOP . The pixels in the TOP are converted to geometries in a grid. The height of the geometries are determined by the brightness of the pixels.

puzzle.summary: The puzzle COMP is an example of Python Extensions in Touchdesigner. A TOP is separated into squares segments and randomized, the user slides the individual pieces to reorganized the TOP . See Extensions for more information.

quadReproject.summary: The quadReproject COMP is used to quickly setup multiple outputs using the Quad Reproject features of the Camera COMP . It is used for LED screens (or any pixel arrays) to render pixel-perfect images, rendered from any viewing location relative to the LED screen. It will output pixel perfect textures for one or multiple screens. For more details about quad reprojection, please refer to the Quad Reprojection page.

radialBlur.summary: The radialBlur COMP blurs a TOP image in a direction specified in the ‘Center Point ’ parameter. Using a GLSL TOP the radialBlur copies the image several times and overlays them to create the blur effect.

recorder.summary: The recorder component is a wrapper for the moviefileout TOP . Its purpose is to simplify the video recording process as a single component. It is recommended to be used as the recording system in larger video applications. To use the recorder: # Connect your video source TOP to the input # Set the output folder and filename parts (supports up to 7 filename segments separated by underscores) # Configure video settings: resolution (custom or from input), frame rate, and frame range # For audio recording, connect an audio CHOP to the Audio CHOP parameter # Click Record Start to begin recording, Record Stop to end (or let it auto-stop if using frame range) Key features: * Automatic filename generation with configurable segments and auto-incrementing index (N parameter) * Support for timeline synchronization and frame-accurate recording * Cook Every Frame option for offline vs real-time recording modes * Custom resolution override capability * Header metadata support via DAT input * Timeline matching utilities for synchronizing recorder settings with project timeline

rgbaBlur.summary: The rgbaBlur COMP blurs the RGBA channels in a TOP individually.

rgbaDelay.summary: The rgbaDelay uses a cacheselect TOP to cache the RGBA channels separately delaying the movement of the selected channel, requires a movieFilein TOP as an input with a video file selected.

rgbContrast.summary: Adjust the contrast in the RGB channels separately.

sceneChanger.summary: This component provides a framework for creating a scene based show where each scene of a show is a component that outputs video from a single TOP called out1. The sceneChanger component references a "Scenes" component from which it will load all "Scene" components it finds inside. The SceneChanger is able to play each component and will cross fade from one component to the next. The workflow of this system, if correctly followed, is implicitly optimized in the sense that scenes that are not being played currently will also not cook.

searchReplace.summary: The searchReplace Component lets you search and (optionally) replace text in operators. The replace operation is undoable .

sharpen.summary: The sharpen COMP uses a Convolve TOP to sharpen an image. The convolve TOP uses kernel processing to perform the sharpen filter. More information about kernel processing can be found on this wikipedia article. [1]

sickEngine.summary: A sickEngine component launches a SICK TOP in a separate process so that more than one SICK sensor can be used in the same project.

signalingClient.summary: The signalingClient COMP is a component that can be used to connect to a signalingServer COMP within TouchDesigner as well as exchange messages with other signalingClients through the signaling server it is connected with.

signalingServer.summary: The signalingServer COMP is a component that can be used to run a signaling server within TouchDesigner.

softenAlpha.summary: Trims and adjust the edges of a TOP by its alpha channel.

solarize.summary: The solarize COMP is an image filter, it adjusts and inverts the brightest and darkest parts of the TOP . The solarize effect is also called the ‘The sabattier effect’ more information can be found on this wikipedia article.

sopRender.summary: sopRender takes a SOP input and renders it with basic lighting and an optional texture map.

splitter.summary: License: Only available in TouchDesigner Educational , TouchDesigner Commercial and TouchDesigner Pro .

stitcher.summary: The stitcher component in the Palette enables the loading and parsing of PTGui project files (PTGui must be licensed to output ASCII or JSON format .pts files). PTGui is a panoramic image stitching software which can be tried and purchased here: https://www.ptgui.com/ . Files saved from the trial version of PTGui will not work as they are encrypted.

superFormula.summary: The superFormula COMP creates a SOP using the 2D and 3D superformula based on equations proposed by Johan Gielis. More information about the superformula equation can be found here .

SVG.summary: The SVG COMP loads and renders SVG ( Scalar Vector Graphics ) files in TouchDesigner. It has functions like Transform order, Translate, Rotate, Scale, Uniform Scale, Pivot.

sweetSpotPreviz.summary: This example illustrates the setup of a so-called Trompe l'Oeil effect where you render a scene from the position of the observer and use the result to re-project it onto a surface. Results created for large format projections, surround environments or tracked monitor setups will seem close to perspectively correct when viewed from the general area of the sweet spot. This technique is especially useful for previsualisation purposes as well as architectural mapping. Tools -> sweetSpot in the palette window is a production-ready component which can be used together with camSchnappr.

sweetSpot.summary: This example illustrates the setup of a so-called Trompe l'Oeil effect where you render a scene from the position of the observer and use the result to re-project it onto a surface. Results created for large format projections, surround environments or tracked monitor setups will seem close to perspectively correct when viewed from the general area of the sweet spot. This technique is especially useful for previsualisation purposes as well as architectural mapping. Tools -> sweetSpot in the palette window is a production-ready component which can be used together with camSchnappr.

synchroCache.summary: Incoming video frames from the first input are cached and indexed according to the frame index coming in on the second input. The second input expects a single channel of the name "frame_stream". The third input expects a single channel of the name "frame_sync". The "frame_sync" channel will typically come over the network via SyncIn CHOP .

synchroFrameIn.summary: The SynchroFrameIn component performs the client role of the TDSynchro system. It configures to receive the frame index from the SynchroFrameOut module located on the server computer. There is one SynchroFrameIn component per client TD application.

synchroFrameOut.summary: Sends a frame index using a SyncCHOP or a TouchOut CHOP .

synchroNDIIn.summary: This NDI In component is a wrapper for the NDIIn TOP . It adds features for extracting timecode and conversion to a frame index as well as signal validation and a backup source for cleaner integration into user friendly systems.

synchroSDIIn.summary: This SDI In component is a wrapper for the videoDeviceIn TOP . It adds features for extracting timecode and conversion to a frame index as well as signal validation and a backup source for cleaner integration into user friendly systems.

synchroVideoOut.summary: Sends out a video stream encoded with a frame index using either SDI or NDI.

tdBitwigPackage.summary: The tdbitwigPackage COMP is responsible for updating the tdBitwig Components in a project. When a new version of the TDBitwig System is released, users can import this Component into their TDBitwig project to bring their current system Components up to date. The version it will update to will match the corresponding version associated with this tdbitwigPackage.

tdPyEnvManager.summary: The TDPyEnvManager is a custom TouchDesigner component that helps manage Python environments as well as Conda environments and helps TouchDesigner users and developers integrate easily and efficiently third party Python libraries that are installed in an external environment.

testGrid.summary: TestGrid is a component under Tools that can be used to create a grid test pattern of any resolution. It offers a rich array of parameters for fine-tuning the graphical elements displayed in your grids.

threadManagerClient.summary: The ThreadManagerClient ( Palette > ThreadManager) is a custom TouchDesigner component designed to rely on the Thread Manager COMP .

threadsMonitor.summary: The ThreadsMonitor ( Palette > ThreadManager) is a small component creating a UI on top of the Thread Manager and can be used as a live debugging tool. It's useful to track what a TDThread is doing, how busy the Thread Manager is, and allows you to monitor a list of all the TDTasks whether they are queued or currently being processed.

twirl.summary: The twirl COMP is a image effect created with a GLSL TOP . It warps a TOP image in a swirling pattern originating from a center point.

virtualFile.summary: This component embeds files within itself so you don't have to refer to, or depend on external files in your project. Instead, the embedded files will be saved within the .toe or .tox files containing this component.

waveformMonitor.summary: The Waveform Monitor displays the color values of each row of the input texture superimposed. For this a pixels color component value is placed on a vertical scale. For example if the red value of a pixel is 0.5, a red pixel is placed at half the height of the display in the pixels horizontal position. More about the usage of Waveform Monitors can be read here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waveform_monitor

webBrowser.summary: The webBrowser component in the palette is an interactive interface to the Web Render TOP . It is currently available on Windows only, in the Commercial, Educational and Pro versions of TouchDesigner.

webRTCPanelRcv.summary: The WebRTC Remote Panel Receiver COMP is a component that can be used to receive a TouchDesigner COMP 's panel over a WebRTC connection from a matching WebRTC Remote Panel .

webRTCPanel.summary: The WebRTC Remote Panel COMP is a component that can be used to share a TouchDesigner component's panel over a WebRTC connection.

webRTC.summary: The WebRTC COMP is a component that can be used to initiate real-time communications (RTC) between TouchDesigner instances and TouchDesigner or WebRTC compatible devices such as most Web browser capable devices, including some IoT devices.

xyScope.summary: The xyScope COMP is an example of an audio visualization. It uses a CHOPto SOP to create a SOP using the input CHOP channels ‘chan1’ and ‘chan2’ as ‘x’ and ‘y’ positions. Any CHOP with two channels can be inputted into the CHOP input of this component.

chunks.text:
Palette: arcBallGeometry
Description: Similar to the Palette:arcBallCamera component, the arcBallGeometry can be used to interactively control a geometry's position in a scene with a fixed camera. The actual functional parts are provided packaged in a complete render setup to better illustrate its usage. You can copy the Geometry COMP arcGeo out of this component and use it in your own render scene or use this as a default interactive render setup. All parameters described here are located on the actual Geometry COMP . The geometry can be rotated ( lclick ), dollied ( mclick ) and translated ( rclick ).

Palette: audioAnalysis
Description: A component to analyze an input audio waveform for its properties such as Low-, Mid-, High-Level, Kick and Snare detection, Rythm, Spectral Centroid as well as Slow- and Fast- Spectral Density.

Palette: audioSet
Description: The audioSet COMP is a visualization and modifier of gain in an Audio File In CHOP .

Palette: autoMediaPlayer
Description: The autoMediaPlayer allows for looping over movies and still images in a specified folder on disc in linear or randomized order. There are options to adjust the output resolution, change the length of still images, as well as set the transition length.

Palette: battery
Description: The Battery COMP monitors the battery or power status of your machine. A Perform CHOP is used to get the power status of your machine.

Palette: bitwigClipSlot
Description: The bitwigClipSlot COMP serves as an interface for controlling clip slot playback and receiving playback information of all clip slots for a given track. Users begin by choosing which track they are interested in; from there they can access the clip slots associated with that track, and subsequently decide to perform actions on a specific clip slot.

Palette: bitwigClip
Description: The bitwigClip COMP serves as an interface for controlling a specific launcher clip in Bitwig's clip launcher context. Users begin by choosing which track they are interested in; then the launcher clip, and subsequently decide to perform actions or read/modify the properties of a given launcher clip.

Palette: bitwigMain
Description: The BitwigMain COMP acts as the central communication and information hub for the TDBitwig System. It is responsible for relaying information between the Bitwig controller extension and the TDBitwig system. There should only be one of these Components present for every Bitwig session the user is communicating to; in most cases this will be one.

Palette: bitwigNote
Description: The bitwigNote COMP allows users to read a live stream of note events on a given Bitwig Track. The incoming notes will correspond to the track's note input source, whether it be output from a midi device or side-chained from another track or device within Bitwig. Users will use the navigation controls to specify which track's input source they want to monitor.

Palette: bitwigRemotesDevice
Description: The bitwigRemotesDevice COMP serves as an interface for accessing and controlling Device Remote Controls in Bitwig. Navigation to controls begins by selecting a track, followed by the device within that track, and ending with the desired page. The 8 Remote Control Parameters on this Component will correspond to the 8 Remote Controls on a single page.

Palette: bitwigRemotesProject
Description: The bitwigRemotesProject COMP serves as an interface for accessing and controlling global Project Remote Controls in Bitwig. Navigation to controls begins by selecting the desired page. The 8 Remote Control Parameters on this Component will correspond to the 8 Remote Controls on a single page.

Palette: bitwigRemotesTrack
Description: The bitwigRemotesTrack COMP serves as an interface for accessing and controlling Track Remote Controls in Bitwig. Navigation to controls begins by selecting a track, followed by the desired page. The 8 Remote Control Parameters on this Component will correspond to the 8 Remote Controls on a single page.

Palette: bitwigSelect
Description: The bitwigSelect COMP enables users to retrieve channel information from the output CHOP of any Bitwig Component in a project. Component search can be filtered by the Object type, and once the ouput CHOP is selected, users can filter specific channels they are interested in. The selection of Components uses python references based on relative paths.

Palette: bitwigSong
Description: The bitwigSong COMP acts as a bi-directional interface for Bitwig's global transport; including timing, playback, and recording functionality. It also stores information about scenes and cues, and supports control for their playback

Palette: bitwigTrack
Description: The BitwigTrack COMP acts as an interface for accessing and controlling the attributes of a given Bitwig Track object. It allows users to choose the specific track they are interested in and perform any actions accordingly.

Palette: blendModes
Description: blendModes is a 2-stream video mixer for exploring and using the 45+ blend modes of the Composite TOP . Connect 2 video inputs, select a Mix Mode (Crossfade by default) and use the X or XY crossfader at the top-middle. On the top right is the blended output of the component.

Palette: bloom
Description: The Bloom COMP is a combination of several image effects that selects and remap the brightest pixels of a TOP . The pixels in the second input TOP are warped around the selection defined by the blur parameters. The blur parameters modifies the selection of the brightest pixels of the input 1 TOP . For example increasing the “Contrast” parameter would reshape the selection to narrow the range of brightness, selecting the brightest pixels. The glow parameters overlays the pixels selected in the blur parameters with the RGB values selected by the “Glow Color” parameters. A Ramp TOP with a color spectrum is the default second input for this COMP . The ramp can be replaced by attaching a TOP to the second input of the component, or the built-one ramp can be edited with the Open Ramp Parameters pulse.

Palette: callbacksHelper
Description: The callbacksHelper Component provides an easy setup for Python callbacks on your custom components. It installs and sets up parameters on your component for the CallbacksExt Extension , and provides a few other usability features.

Palette: cameraViewport
Description: The cameraViewport component is a modified Camera COMP that can be used to interactively control a camera's position/rotation in a 3D scene. It can be used instead of the default Camera COMP . Its viewer is an interactive Panel containing a rendered image from any Render TOP .

Palette: camSchnappr
Description: camSchnappr is a interactive mapping application entirely inspired by MAPAMOK, created by Kyle McDonald at the YCAM Interlab. With Kyle publishing the source code for MAPAMOK, we had a chance to look at it and convert it into TouchDesigner. For the original tool and source have a look here :

Palette: changeColor
Description: The changeColor COMP selects a color using the RGB values in the “Color to change” parameter and modifies the selected color using the HSV parameter values. The changeColor COMP can output a color corrected TOP image or an alpha mask.

Palette: changeToColor
Description: The changeToColor COMP selects a color with the “From Color” parameter and transforms it to the color specified in the “To Color” parameter. The COMP changes the hue, saturation and value of “From Color” to the “To Color”, the tolerance of the transformation can be adjusted with the hue, saturation and value tolerance parameters. The changeToColor COMP outputs a color corrected TOP image or an alpha mask.

Palette: checker
Description: The Checker COMP uses two RGBA colors to create a checker pattern TOP image.

Palette: colorThreshold
Description: Modifies a chosen color channel from a TOP by multiplying the original input by an inverse of the selected color channels.

Palette: compareComp
Description: This compares two components. It first identifies nodes that are unique to either of the two components.

Palette: convolve
Description: The convolve COMP is a simple input interface for the Convolve TOP . The three parameters in "Line 1", "Line 2" and "Line 3" are a convolution matrix which is used to create image effects like sharpen, blur and edge detection. See Convolve TOP for more details.

Palette: cornerPinPOP
Description: The Corner Pin POP component allows for perspective or bilinear deformation of a POP . It can be best compared in functionality to the Corner Pin TOP . It uses OpenCV's . findHomography () function to calculate the perspective transform matrix.

Palette: cornerPinSOP
Description: The Corner Pin SOP component allows for perspective or bilinear deformation of a SOP . It can be best compared in functionality to the Corner Pin TOP .

Palette: cppParsTemplateGen
Description: The CppParsTemplateGen COMP can be used to quickly generate parameters.cpp and parameters.h files and kickstart your Custom Operator projects. The parameters.cpp and parameters.h files are used to define the Custom Parameters on Custom OPs.

Palette: customAttributes
Description: This component helps with adding custom attributes to a SOP via python scripting. It duplicates functionality found in the Point SOP , Vertex SOP and Primitive SOP but can still be helpful when trying to find out how to add attributes via python.

Palette: debugControl
Description: This component facilitates experimentation with and control of the builtin debug statement. It is an operator based interface for the debug module .

Palette: dent
Description: Palette:dent Ext

Palette: dilate
Description: The Dialate COMP is an example of morphological image processing created with a GLSL TOP . This COMP has two types of processesses dilate and erode. The dialate effect adds pixels to the edge of groups of similar pixels. The erode effect removes pixels on the edges of groups of similar pixels. More information on morphological operation can be found on this wikipedia article .

Palette: domeViewer
Description: A component designed to previsualize dome specific content and / or generate a dome shell (in POPs) to be used to design dome specific content. This is based out of Fred Trétout's (anodin) InsideDome. The attached file is a sample using the Dome Viewer to generate dome specific effects. File:DomeViewer LinesDemo.toe

Palette: equalizer
Description: Palette:equalizer Ext

Palette: feedbackEdge
Description: The Feedback Edge COMP is a feedback image effect created using a Feedback, Blur, Edge, Level and Hsvadj TOPs.

Palette: feedback
Description: The feedback COMP is an example of the feedback TOP . The feedback COMP is used to create a fake motion blur by not clearing the buffer. For more information see the Feedback TOP . See OP Snippets and the Examples section for some networks detailing to use the Feedback TOP .

Palette: firmata
Description: Firmata is an Arduino sketch allowing for configuration and control of the Arduino using messages formatted as MIDI.

Palette: gestureCapture
Description: The gestureCapture COMP records movement and then displays the curves as (1) rendered lines and/or (2) a dot following the curves and/or (3) free-hand drawn shapes. The outputs of gestureCapture are (a) the rendered image, (b) a SOP containing all paths as polygonal lines, (c) a CHOP containing the merged lines and (d) number of lines. Internally you can access a CHOP and a SOP for every gesture recorded.

Palette: graphPlot
Description: graphPlot creates 2D graphs with data points or curves plotted over XY grids with controls over units, ranges, divisions and labels.

Palette: histogram
Description: Palette:histogram Ext

Palette: hsvBlur
Description: The hsvBlur COMP converts a TOP to use the HSV color space then blurs the HSV channels individually.

Palette: imageSearch
Description: ImageSearch uses the Custom Search interface of Google to find images which match the given search term.

Palette: julia
Description: The Julia COMP is a mapping of the Julia fractal set, more information about the Julia set can be found here .

Palette: kantanMapper
Description: Kantan Mapper is an interface overlaid on-top of an actual projection to enable the drawing, creation and placement of various shapes onto real life objects and the possibility to assign TOP Image Nodes to these shapes and masks.

Palette: kantanUVHelper
Description: The kantanUVHelper is a utility tool to make use of knatanMapper's second UV Map output. The UV map has a texture id encoded in the blue channel and kantanUVHelper uses this to map the right input texture to the correct area. The benefit of this is that kantanMapper can be disabled or even deleted if the UV map from the second output is saved or locked.

Palette: kinectCalibration
Description: THe kinectCalibrate component allows for calculating the intrinsics and extrinsics of a projector in relation to a Kinect device making it possible to project onto the scene captured by the kinect. The theory is the same as camSchnappr. Where camSchnappr has a 3D Object that mirrors a physical object, this component uses the point cloud as a real-world reference. Projecting a checkboard, the component detects the corners in the color image of the kinect and retireves the 3D positions of these checkerboard corners from the poincloud data. Both sets of data (2D positions of corners in projector image and 3D pos of corners in pointcloud) are the variables needed by openCV's calibrateCamera function to solve for the position of the camera - in this case the projector. The Kinect's position in this setup is assumed to be at the root.

Palette: kinectPointcloud
Description: The kinectPointCloud COMP generates a point cloud using depth textures and color data. The point cloud texture is a 32-bit floating point texture where RGB pixel values are XYZ pixel values relative to the color camera, in meters. By default the COMP uses the Kinect device as an input, to use other depth cameras connect the color and depth textures to input 1 and input 2 of the component. Using the Kinect with this COMP is only supported on Windows OS with the Kinect for Windows hardware. To use a Kinect 2 device you need to install the SDK or runtime from here .

Palette: leapPaint
Description: The leapPaint COMP uses the Leap Motion device and user's hands as a controller to draw in a TOP . There are two API versions for the Leap Motion device, some features are not supported on “Version 4 Orion” API for example “swipe”. More information on using the Leap Motion in TouchDesigner can be found on this page .

Palette: lightTunnel
Description: The lightTunnel COMP uses a GLSL Multi TOP to remap and distort the pixels in an input TOP . The lightTunnel selects a circle in the input TOP and extends the color of the edges to stretch across the rest of the image.

Palette: lister
Description: The lister custom Component builds on List COMP to make a multi-featured tool for listing complex data. Formatting of the lister is set up in the config Component , which starts out docked to the lister. Data can be provided via Python, parameter or table. Complex data and effects can be achieved using callbacks . NOTE: Many of lister's features require a basic knowledge of Python.

Palette: logger
Description: Starting with the first build released publicly on the 2023.10k branch , TouchDesigner now comes with its own Logger COMP .

Palette: mandelbrot
Description: The Mandelbrot COMP is a mapping of the Mandlebrot fractal set, more information about the Mandelbrot set can be found here .

Palette: materialDesignIcons
Description: The materialDesignIcons component allows for browsing and searching of the MaterialDesignIcons font. The currently included version contains over 6000 icons.

Palette: mesh
Description: The Mesh COMP creates a SOP based on the selected parametric equation in the "shape" parameter.

Palette: monochrome
Description: The monochrome COMP converts a TOP to grayscale colors. The intensity of each color channel can be adjusted with the red, blue and green parameters.

Palette: motionSense
Description: The motionSense COMP detects motion in a TOP texture or a camera device. This COMP outputs a 0 or 1 in a CHOP if there is movement detected.

Palette: movieBlender
Description: Palette/movieBlender Ext

Palette: movieEngine
Description: The movieEngine Palette component is a UI-less moviePlayer , and is a multi-purpose tool for playing, controlling and examining movies. It can be used standalone or embedded in TouchDesigner applications. Unless otherwise noted, movieEngine features are also part of moviePlayer.

Palette: moviePlayer
Description: The moviePlayer Palette component (and the UI-less movieEngine ) is a multi-purpose tool for playing, controlling and examining movies. It can be used standalone or embedded in TouchDesigner applications.

Palette: moviePlaylist
Description: The moviePlaylist is able to loop over images or movies selected via a folder or specified via a Table DAT given as an input. It offers some control over playback, blend- and end- behaviour.

Palette: multiLevel
Description: By clicking and dragging around the UI, multiLevel outputs a single channel of multiple samples, or multiple channels one sample each.

Palette: multiMix
Description: Palette:multiMix Ext

Palette: noise
Description: The Noise COMP generates a variety of noise patterns based on the selected noise type. More information about how the noise patterns are calculated can be found on this blog by Brian Sharpe .

Palette: onScreenKeyboard
Description: Palette:onScreenKeyboard Ext

Palette: opBrowser
Description: The opBrowser Custom Component provides a range of network browsing, viewing, and parameter editing capabilities. It can be used as a standalone browser or embedded into other UI's. All configuration features are controlled by custom parameters and can be made accessible or not in the browser UI.

Palette: operatorPath
Description: The operatorPath Component is a full-featured UI element for navigating operator paths in TouchDesigner networks.

Palette: opticalFlow
Description: This finds apparent motion and puts the left-right motion in the red channel of the output TOP , and the bottom-top motion in the green channel.

Palette: particlesGpu
Description: particlesGpu is a compute shader based particle system.

Palette: pixelate
Description: The pixelate COMP turns a TOP to a color bitmap by adjust the resolution.

Palette: pixelRelocator
Description: The pixelRelocator COMP repositions the pixels from the first input TOP to a position determined by the second input TOP . The positions are determined by the color channels selected in the Horizontal source and vertical source parameter.

Palette: pointillize
Description: The pointillize COMP draws a point at each pixel and colors the point with the corresponding color, creating a pointillism effect.

Palette: pointRender
Description: Renders a point cloud from the data stored in the input textures. The first input should contain XYZ data stored in the RGB channels with the alpha channel set to one for pixels that contain valid points. The second input can contain optional color data that will be applied to each point. The Normalize Colors parameter can be used to rescale colors that are outside the expected zero to one range.

Palette: popDialog
Description: The popDialog custom Component builds on Container COMP to make an easy-to-use pop-up dialog for basic notifications and queries. The dialog can be set up using parameters or script.

Palette: popMenu
Description: The popMenu custom Component builds on Container COMP to make an easy-to-use pop-up menu that can be used as a context menu, in dropdown controls, or for other menu needs. The dialog can be set up using parameters or script. Multi-level menus can be created through scripting. Custom formatting of popMenu is set up in the internal config Component . A callback system is provided for more complex needs.

Palette: probe
Description: Probe is a visual performance monitor for TouchDesigner processes, measuring CPU times (circles), and GPU times (diamonds), plus, via the selector on the left, CPU memory, GPU memory and the number of children in a component.

Palette: pushPins
Description: The pushPins COMP creates a pushpin effect using a TOP . The pixels in the TOP are converted to geometries in a grid. The height of the geometries are determined by the brightness of the pixels.

Palette: puzzle
Description: The puzzle COMP is an example of Python Extensions in Touchdesigner. A TOP is separated into squares segments and randomized, the user slides the individual pieces to reorganized the TOP . See Extensions for more information.

Palette: quadReproject
Description: The quadReproject COMP is used to quickly setup multiple outputs using the Quad Reproject features of the Camera COMP . It is used for LED screens (or any pixel arrays) to render pixel-perfect images, rendered from any viewing location relative to the LED screen. It will output pixel perfect textures for one or multiple screens. For more details about quad reprojection, please refer to the Quad Reprojection page.

Palette: radialBlur
Description: The radialBlur COMP blurs a TOP image in a direction specified in the ‘Center Point ’ parameter. Using a GLSL TOP the radialBlur copies the image several times and overlays them to create the blur effect.

Palette: recorder
Description: The recorder component is a wrapper for the moviefileout TOP . Its purpose is to simplify the video recording process as a single component. It is recommended to be used as the recording system in larger video applications. To use the recorder: # Connect your video source TOP to the input # Set the output folder and filename parts (supports up to 7 filename segments separated by underscores) # Configure video settings: resolution (custom or from input), frame rate, and frame range # For audio recording, connect an audio CHOP to the Audio CHOP parameter # Click Record Start to begin recording, Record Stop to end (or let it auto-stop if using frame range) Key features: * Automatic filename generation with configurable segments and auto-incrementing index (N parameter) * Support for timeline synchronization and frame-accurate recording * Cook Every Frame option for offline vs real-time recording modes * Custom resolution override capability * Header metadata support via DAT input * Timeline matching utilities for synchronizing recorder settings with project timeline

Palette: rgbaBlur
Description: The rgbaBlur COMP blurs the RGBA channels in a TOP individually.

Palette: rgbaDelay
Description: The rgbaDelay uses a cacheselect TOP to cache the RGBA channels separately delaying the movement of the selected channel, requires a movieFilein TOP as an input with a video file selected.

Palette: rgbContrast
Description: Adjust the contrast in the RGB channels separately.

Palette: sceneChanger
Description: This component provides a framework for creating a scene based show where each scene of a show is a component that outputs video from a single TOP called out1. The sceneChanger component references a "Scenes" component from which it will load all "Scene" components it finds inside. The SceneChanger is able to play each component and will cross fade from one component to the next. The workflow of this system, if correctly followed, is implicitly optimized in the sense that scenes that are not being played currently will also not cook.

Palette: searchReplace
Description: The searchReplace Component lets you search and (optionally) replace text in operators. The replace operation is undoable .

Palette: search
Description: Palette:search Ext

Palette: sharpen
Description: The sharpen COMP uses a Convolve TOP to sharpen an image. The convolve TOP uses kernel processing to perform the sharpen filter. More information about kernel processing can be found on this wikipedia article. [1]

Palette: sickEngine
Description: A sickEngine component launches a SICK TOP in a separate process so that more than one SICK sensor can be used in the same project.

Palette: signalingClient
Description: The signalingClient COMP is a component that can be used to connect to a signalingServer COMP within TouchDesigner as well as exchange messages with other signalingClients through the signaling server it is connected with.

Palette: signalingServer
Description: The signalingServer COMP is a component that can be used to run a signaling server within TouchDesigner.

Palette: softenAlpha
Description: Trims and adjust the edges of a TOP by its alpha channel.

Palette: solarize
Description: The solarize COMP is an image filter, it adjusts and inverts the brightest and darkest parts of the TOP . The solarize effect is also called the ‘The sabattier effect’ more information can be found on this wikipedia article.

Palette: sopRender
Description: sopRender takes a SOP input and renders it with basic lighting and an optional texture map.

Palette: splitter
Description: License: Only available in TouchDesigner Educational , TouchDesigner Commercial and TouchDesigner Pro .

Palette: stitcher
Description: The stitcher component in the Palette enables the loading and parsing of PTGui project files (PTGui must be licensed to output ASCII or JSON format .pts files). PTGui is a panoramic image stitching software which can be tried and purchased here: https://www.ptgui.com/ . Files saved from the trial version of PTGui will not work as they are encrypted.

Palette: superFormula
Description: The superFormula COMP creates a SOP using the 2D and 3D superformula based on equations proposed by Johan Gielis. More information about the superformula equation can be found here .

Palette: SVG
Description: The SVG COMP loads and renders SVG ( Scalar Vector Graphics ) files in TouchDesigner. It has functions like Transform order, Translate, Rotate, Scale, Uniform Scale, Pivot.

Palette: sweetSpotPreviz
Description: This example illustrates the setup of a so-called Trompe l'Oeil effect where you render a scene from the position of the observer and use the result to re-project it onto a surface. Results created for large format projections, surround environments or tracked monitor setups will seem close to perspectively correct when viewed from the general area of the sweet spot. This technique is especially useful for previsualisation purposes as well as architectural mapping. Tools -> sweetSpot in the palette window is a production-ready component which can be used together with camSchnappr.

Palette: sweetSpot
Description: This example illustrates the setup of a so-called Trompe l'Oeil effect where you render a scene from the position of the observer and use the result to re-project it onto a surface. Results created for large format projections, surround environments or tracked monitor setups will seem close to perspectively correct when viewed from the general area of the sweet spot. This technique is especially useful for previsualisation purposes as well as architectural mapping. Tools -> sweetSpot in the palette window is a production-ready component which can be used together with camSchnappr.

Palette: synchroCache
Description: Incoming video frames from the first input are cached and indexed according to the frame index coming in on the second input. The second input expects a single channel of the name "frame_stream". The third input expects a single channel of the name "frame_sync". The "frame_sync" channel will typically come over the network via SyncIn CHOP .

Palette: synchroClient
Description: Palette:synchroClient Ext

Palette: synchroFrameIn
Description: The SynchroFrameIn component performs the client role of the TDSynchro system. It configures to receive the frame index from the SynchroFrameOut module located on the server computer. There is one SynchroFrameIn component per client TD application.

Palette: synchroFrameOut
Description: Sends a frame index using a SyncCHOP or a TouchOut CHOP .

Palette: synchroNDIIn
Description: This NDI In component is a wrapper for the NDIIn TOP . It adds features for extracting timecode and conversion to a frame index as well as signal validation and a backup source for cleaner integration into user friendly systems.

Palette: synchroSDIIn
Description: This SDI In component is a wrapper for the videoDeviceIn TOP . It adds features for extracting timecode and conversion to a frame index as well as signal validation and a backup source for cleaner integration into user friendly systems.

Palette: synchroVideoOut
Description: Sends out a video stream encoded with a frame index using either SDI or NDI.

Palette: tdBitwigPackage
Description: The tdbitwigPackage COMP is responsible for updating the tdBitwig Components in a project. When a new version of the TDBitwig System is released, users can import this Component into their TDBitwig project to bring their current system Components up to date. The version it will update to will match the corresponding version associated with this tdbitwigPackage.

Palette: tdPyEnvManager
Description: The TDPyEnvManager is a custom TouchDesigner component that helps manage Python environments as well as Conda environments and helps TouchDesigner users and developers integrate easily and efficiently third party Python libraries that are installed in an external environment.

Palette: testGrid
Description: TestGrid is a component under Tools that can be used to create a grid test pattern of any resolution. It offers a rich array of parameters for fine-tuning the graphical elements displayed in your grids.

Palette: threadManagerClient
Description: The ThreadManagerClient ( Palette > ThreadManager) is a custom TouchDesigner component designed to rely on the Thread Manager COMP .

Palette: threadsMonitor
Description: The ThreadsMonitor ( Palette > ThreadManager) is a small component creating a UI on top of the Thread Manager and can be used as a live debugging tool. It's useful to track what a TDThread is doing, how busy the Thread Manager is, and allows you to monitor a list of all the TDTasks whether they are queued or currently being processed.

Palette: twirl
Description: The twirl COMP is a image effect created with a GLSL TOP . It warps a TOP image in a swirling pattern originating from a center point.

Palette: vectorScope
Description: Palette:vectorScope Ext

Palette: virtualFile
Description: This component embeds files within itself so you don't have to refer to, or depend on external files in your project. Instead, the embedded files will be saved within the .toe or .tox files containing this component.

Palette: waveformMonitor
Description: The Waveform Monitor displays the color values of each row of the input texture superimposed. For this a pixels color component value is placed on a vertical scale. For example if the red value of a pixel is 0.5, a red pixel is placed at half the height of the display in the pixels horizontal position. More about the usage of Waveform Monitors can be read here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waveform_monitor

Palette: webBrowser
Description: The webBrowser component in the palette is an interactive interface to the Web Render TOP . It is currently available on Windows only, in the Commercial, Educational and Pro versions of TouchDesigner.

Palette: webRTCPanelRcv
Description: The WebRTC Remote Panel Receiver COMP is a component that can be used to receive a TouchDesigner COMP 's panel over a WebRTC connection from a matching WebRTC Remote Panel .

Palette: webRTCPanel
Description: The WebRTC Remote Panel COMP is a component that can be used to share a TouchDesigner component's panel over a WebRTC connection.

Palette: webRTC
Description: The WebRTC COMP is a component that can be used to initiate real-time communications (RTC) between TouchDesigner instances and TouchDesigner or WebRTC compatible devices such as most Web browser capable devices, including some IoT devices.

Palette: xyScope
Description: The xyScope COMP is an example of an audio visualization. It uses a CHOPto SOP to create a SOP using the input CHOP channels ‘chan1’ and ‘chan2’ as ‘x’ and ‘y’ positions. Any CHOP with two channels can be inputted into the CHOP input of this component.
